headline.purpose: Window title and current connection state.

body.purpose: Status, controller identity, and security consequences.

motion.name: state-change
motion.purpose: State feedback only; native Windows UI may render instantly when reduced motion is enabled.

narrative.overview: One clear indicator on a well-made physical device: easy to find, unambiguous when it changes, and otherwise silent.
keyCharacteristics: Status-first hierarchy with plain-language recovery detail
Restrained color with explicit semantic states
Compact trusted-device management without nested navigation

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: At most one saturated status or action color dominates a view. Status is always paired with a written label.

rules.name: The Plain Label Rule
rules.body: Buttons name the action and object. Never use an unexplained OK for a security action.

rules.name: The System Owns the Shadow Rule
rules.body: Never draw additional card shadows inside the native window.

dos: Do keep the current connection state visible at the top of the window.
Do identify trusted controllers fully before revocation.
Do preserve keyboard access, DPI scaling, high contrast, and native focus behavior.
donts: Don't build an enterprise administration console.
Don't use neon gaming or streaming overlays.
Don't add decorative security gauges, glowing maps, fear-driven warnings, glass surfaces, or gradient text.
Don't hide background behavior or label irreversible actions only OK or Yes.
